channel.title: The Grant Desk - AI & Safety, next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: AI & Safety, next 30 days.

item.title: AGI Governance Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Johns Hopkins University, School of Government and Policy (SOGP)

Location: Hopkins Bloomberg Center, Washington, D.C. (in-person only)

Award: Fully funded: travel, accommodation, and meals covered.

Duration: Three-week in-person intensive, 8-25 September 2026

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

The AGI Governance Fellowship is the inaugural three-week intensive run by Johns Hopkins University's School of Government and Policy (SOGP) to develop the next generation of leaders in AI governance. COVERS: frontier AI legislation at state and federal levels; building societal resilience amid rapid capability growth; democratic practices in AGI alignment; and designing new governing institutions for the AGI era. WHO SHOULD APPLY: early-career researchers and practitioners (within roughly eight years of undergrad) with solid foundations in AI governance, including congressional staffers, AGI company employees, recent CS or policy PhDs, and nonprofit/civil society professionals; a postgraduate degree or equivalent experience is required. This is a capstone program for rising leaders, not an intro course. FUNDING: travel, accommodation, and meals are fully covered. DATES: 8-25 September 2026 at the Hopkins Bloomberg Center in Washington, D.C. (in-person only). DEADLINE: apply by 15 July 2026 for full consideration; rolling applications accepted. APPLY: https://sogp.jh.edu/agi-governance-fellowship/

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Horizon Institute for Public Service: AI Rapid Response Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Horizon Institute for Public Service

Location: Washington, DC area, United States (in-person; full-time placements in U.S. executive branch offices).

Award: Fellow track: USD 170,000 to 200,000+, plus an additional stipend for benefits. Senior Fellow track: from USD 250,000+, plus a benefits stipend, commensurate with the seniority of the placement.

Duration: One year full-time, with the opportunity for renewal.

Deadline: 22 Jul 2026

The AI Rapid Response Fellowship is a focused effort by the Horizon Institute for Public Service to close the AI security talent gap in the U.S. federal government. Horizon is recruiting a specialized cohort of exceptional technical and policy talent to work full-time in executive branch offices on AI security-related issues, with placements beginning as soon as possible. TRACKS: the Fellow track is for candidates with technical or policy expertise relevant to AI security, typically 2-6+ years of full-time relevant experience; the Senior Fellow track is for candidates with substantial prior experience, typically 10-15+ years in relevant technical, policy, or leadership roles. FOCUS: AI governance, AI security, policy, and public service. COMPENSATION: Fellow track USD 170,000-200,000+ plus a benefits stipend; Senior Fellow track from USD 250,000+ plus a benefits stipend, commensurate with the seniority of the placement. DURATION: one year full-time, with the opportunity for renewal, based in the Washington, DC area. ELIGIBILITY: U.S. citizenship is required. An existing security clearance, or eligibility to obtain one, is helpful but not required. DEADLINE: applications are due July 22, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Google Data Center Community AI Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Google (with Watson Institute)

Location: Google Data Center communities, United States. Fellowship delivered with mentorship and training.

Award: Fully funded fellowship including AI training, mentorship, Google resources, and a USD 1,000 community development stipend. No application fee.

Duration: Fellowship program with AI training and mentorship (dates per program).

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

The Google Data Center Community AI Fellowship is a fully funded fellowship for community leaders and entrepreneurs using AI or technology to create local impact in Google Data Center communities. Fellows receive AI training, mentorship, access to Google resources, and a USD 1,000 community development stipend to support projects that deliver local impact. WHO IT'S FOR: community leaders and entrepreneurs applying AI or technology to community-level challenges in Google Data Center communities. WHAT'S OFFERED: AI training, mentorship, Google resources, and a USD 1,000 stipend. PRIORITY DEADLINE: 12 July 2026. TO APPLY: submit the application via the Watson Institute program page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Genesis Mission AI Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Horizon Institute for Public Service (with Renaissance Philanthropy, SeedAI, and Fulcrum Science)

Location: United States. Fellows are forward deployed to US Department of Energy national labs (initial partners: Idaho National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory). Remote or in-person; on-site travel at least once a quarter. Must be eligible to work in the US (no visa sponsorship; US green card holders eligible).

Award: Prorated total annual compensation of USD 200,000.

Duration: 9-12 months, starting fall 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Horizon Institute for Public Service, Renaissance Philanthropy, SeedAI, and Fulcrum Science are partnering to launch a fellowship that embeds technical AI talent across the US Department of Energy national labs to advance the goals of the Genesis Mission, DOE's flagship science and technology initiative launched in late 2025. The Genesis Mission identifies 26 national science and technology challenges critical to US leadership and envisions a national platform connecting supercomputers, experimental facilities, AI systems, and unique datasets, with DOE's 17 national labs playing a key role. Genesis Mission AI Fellows are forward deployed to DOE national labs to bring frontier AI expertise to high-priority scientific challenges. As core members of the technical staff on a Genesis Mission project, fellows work alongside national lab scientists and partners and apply AI to national priorities, from building cutting-edge models of plasma dynamics in pursuit of controlled fusion to advancing state-of-the-art models for foundational materials discovery. Initial national lab partners include Idaho National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, and the Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory; additional partners may be announced later. COMPENSATION/DURATION: 9-12 months starting fall 2026, with a prorated total annual compensation of USD 200,000; fellows are expected to travel to their national lab site at least once a quarter but may work on-site if they prefer. ELIGIBILITY: competitive applicants should have at least 2 years of industry experience building AI models or agents (those with industry experience building AI applications or data infrastructure will also be considered); scientific domain expertise is not required, but any placement will involve some training and study in a specific scientific discipline or energy topic, supported by expert colleagues at the host institution. All fellows must be eligible to work in the United States for the duration of the placement; US employment visas cannot be sponsored, but US permanent residents (green card holders) are eligible. APPLICATION: initial form requires a CV or resume (max 2 pages) and short-answer questions on motivations and experiences (max 200 words); successful applicants complete interviews through partner organizations and national labs, and selected fellows are matched to partner labs by scientific domain and availability. The review uses evidence-based best practices including blind grading of application materials. TIMELINE: application deadline 31 July 2026; final fellow selection August-September 2026; expected start fall 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Center for Humane Technology: Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship (Inaugural Cohort)
item.description:
Organisation: Center for Humane Technology (CHT)

Location: Remote, 10 hours/week overlapping with EST required.

Award: $30,000 fixed stipend paid in three milestone-based tranches. No application fee.

Duration: 6 months: September 14, 2026 through March 18, 2027. 15 hours per week.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

CHT's inaugural Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship places 3 fellows (one per focus area) in a 6-month, part-time program to develop rigorous public-impact research and writing on how AI is reshaping society. Fellows choose from 5 focus areas: (1) AI and Cognition — what AI does to human judgment, critical thinking, and intellectual independence; (2) AI and Relationships — AI's effects on intimacy, companionship, and social bonds; (3) AI and Surveillance — how AI enables monitoring, tracking, and control; (4) AI and Spirituality — AI's intersection with meaning, transcendence, and religious practice; (5) Inside the Machine: AI From a Technologist's Perspective. Up to 2 focus areas per application. DELIVERABLES: one flagship research piece, report, or long-form essay for public impact; 1-3 op-eds (placed in target outlets or CHT Substack); one podcast appearance (Your Undivided Attention or equivalent); cohort meetings and bi-weekly check-ins. SUPPORT: bi-weekly guidance from Senior Director of Strategy and Impact; editorial, communications and graphic design support; media training and PR placement support; CHT email address and Slack access; listing on CHT's public fellows page. WHO SHOULD APPLY: mid-career researchers (psychology, sociology, philosophy, cognitive science, communications, STS), policy analysts, journalists covering tech and society, civil society advocates, or technologists with insider perspective. Strong preference for those with a public writing track record (op-eds, Substack, peer-reviewed work) and some public presence. HOW TO APPLY: 1-2 page research proposal per topic (up to 2), CV/resume (2 pages max), 2-minute video explaining fit, writing sample. Deadline: Sunday 12 July 2026, 11:59pm EST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI)

Location: Washington, DC, USA; in-person, full-time (part-time possible for DC-based think tank or government staff).

Award: $21,000 stipend for the three-month term, plus travel support to DC and weekday lunch provided. No visa sponsorship. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, 18 January to 9 April 2027.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

GovAI's DC Winter Fellowship is a three-month bipartisan in-person fellowship in Washington, DC, designed for individuals seeking to enter or advance in US AI governance and policy. The program emphasises bipartisan engagement, rigorous analysis, and practical policy relevance, with a focus on how AI policy is shaped within the US political system. Fellows conduct independent research on a topic of their choice with mentorship from a GovAI supervisor; the first two weeks explore the AI policy landscape before a research proposal is finalised. Outputs may include a report, white paper, journal article, op-ed, or blog post targeted at an audience relevant to AI policy. Fellows also attend expert Q&As, skills workshops, seminars, work-in-progress meetings, and social events, and are encouraged to discuss follow-on career opportunities. GovAI alumni have gone on to roles in US government bodies (NSC, OSTP, Senate, Department of Commerce, DHS); think tanks (CSIS, CSET, CNAS, RAND, Heritage Foundation, FAI); AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind); and universities (Yale, Stanford, MIT). AMOUNT: $21,000 stipend for three months, plus travel support to DC and weekday lunch. Part-time fellowship at a pro-rated salary available for DC-based think tank researchers, government staffers, or professionals doing AI governance work; pay accommodations possible. ELIGIBILITY: candidates must have the right to work in the US; no visa sponsorship. Open to a wide range of backgrounds (government, academia, industry, civil society); promising undergraduates welcome; no strict degree requirements. Relevant areas include public policy, political science, engineering, computer science, economics, biosecurity, cybersecurity, China studies, and risk management. SELECTION CRITERIA: quality and clarity of written work, relevant expertise, judgement, and team fit. APPLICATION PROCESS: (1) written application and automated abstract reasoning assessment; (2) paid remote work test (August 2026); (3) remote interview and reference checks (September 2026); decisions communicated October 2026. Application deadline not yet publicly stated; applications open June 2026 -- check programme page for the deadline.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 - Research Track
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI)

Location: London, UK; in-person, full-time.

Award: £12,000 salary for the three-month term, plus travel support to London and weekday lunch provided. Visa sponsorship (3-month temporary work visa) available. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, 18 January to 9 April 2027.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

GovAI's UK Winter Fellowship (Research Track) is a three-month in-person fellowship in London for researchers and policy professionals seeking to enter or advance careers in AI governance and policy. Fellows conduct independent research on a topic of their choice, paired with a GovAI supervisor from the team or network; the first two weeks are spent exploring the AI governance landscape before a research proposal is finalised. Research outputs may include a report, white paper, journal article, op-ed, or blog post targeted at an audience relevant to AI governance. Alongside the project, fellows attend expert Q&As, weekly seminars, skills workshops, work-in-progress meetings, and social events, and are encouraged to discuss follow-on career opportunities with GovAI's team and network. GovAI alumni have gone on to roles in US, UK, and EU government; leading AI companies (Anthropic, DeepMind, OpenAI); top think tanks (CSET, RAND); and leading universities (Oxford, Cambridge). AMOUNT: £12,000 salary for three months, plus travel support to London and weekday lunch. Visa sponsorship for a 3-month temporary work visa is available; successful visa applicants must remain in their country of application while the visa is processed. ELIGIBILITY: open to a wide range of backgrounds (government, academia, industry, civil society); no strict degree requirements; relevant areas include public policy, political science, computer science, economics, biosecurity, cybersecurity, China studies, and risk management. Senior candidates who need accommodations should email fellowships@governance.ai with subject line 'senior candidate winter fellowship 2027' and a CV. SELECTION CRITERIA: quality and clarity of written work, relevant expertise, judgement in prioritising research directions, and team fit (intellectual honesty, openness to feedback, comfort with uncertainty). APPLICATION PROCESS: (1) written application and automated abstract reasoning assessment; (2) paid remote work test (August 2026); (3) remote interview and reference checks (September 2026); decisions communicated October 2026. Deadline: 12 July 2026, 23:59 BST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Claude Corps Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Anthropic (funded); CodePath (employer of record); Social Finance (evaluation)

Location: USA; in-person at host nonprofit organizations across America (placement location varies; relocation support available).

Award: $85,000 full-time annual salary plus benefits, expansive Claude token budget, and relocation support as needed. No application fee.

Duration: 12 months.

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026

Claude Corps is a national fellowship program from Anthropic placing early-career fellows full-time at nonprofits across America for one year, with the goal of extending AI's benefits to communities and building fellows' AI skills. Anthropic funds the program and provides Claude expertise; CodePath (America's largest collegiate CS education provider) is the fellows' official employer of record and leads programming; Social Finance leads measurement and evaluation. AMOUNT: $85,000 full-time salary plus benefits; expansive Claude token budget; relocation support as needed. FELLOW EXPERIENCE: intensive Claude training at the start; five hours of ongoing weekly training throughout; support from a CodePath mentor; Anthropic office hours for technical questions; and professional guidance from the host organization manager. SAMPLE HOST ORGANIZATIONS: Braven (Chicago), Code the Dream (Durham NC), Heartland Forward (Bentonville AR), Montgomery County Food Bank (Conroe TX), Reef Environmental Education Foundation (Key Largo FL), StriveTogether (Cincinnati), YMCA of Greater Charlotte, and more than 400 nonprofits total. ELIGIBILITY: anyone over 18 with under two years of full-time work experience, regardless of educational background; must be authorized to work in the US; must be comfortable working with Claude and willing to relocate if necessary. COHORTS: Cohort 1 (100 fellows, starts October 2026) -- applications close 17 July 2026; Cohort 2 (starts January 2027) and Cohort 3 (starts August 2027) -- rolling applications. Apply at https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-corps.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World (Joint Funding Call)
item.description:
Organisation: Schmidt Sciences, Google DeepMind, the Advanced Research & Invention Agency (ARIA), the Cooperative AI Foundation, and Google.org

Location: Global; open worldwide, with collaborations across geographic boundaries encouraged.

Award: Two funding tiers (applicants may apply to either or both), projects 1 to 2 years. Tier 1: up to USD 300,000 for exploratory research, pilot studies, or focused technical investigations. Tier 2: USD 300,000 to USD 1,000,000 for more ambitious or collaborative projects. For projects funded by Schmidt Sciences, indirect costs must be at or below 10%. No application fee.

Duration: 1 to 2 years

Deadline: 08 Aug 2026

A joint philanthropic funding call by Schmidt Sciences, Google DeepMind, ARIA, the Cooperative AI Foundation and Google.org to catalyse the foundational scientific research needed to understand, evaluate and control risks emerging from large-scale ecosystems of interacting AI agents deployed by multiple actors (multi-principal, multi-agent settings). The call argues that focusing only on the safety and alignment of individual models is insufficient, and seeks system-level approaches. RESEARCH AGENDA (four clusters): (1) Sandboxes and Testbeds, realistic, reproducible multi-agent environments for studying populations of frontier-model agents; (2) The Science of Agent Networks, how collective capabilities emerge and scale, how networks fail, and how dangerous population-level properties can be detected; (3) Strengthening Agent Infrastructure, evaluating and stress-testing primitives such as identity, verifiability, reputation, communication and commitment; (4) Multi-Agent Oversight and Control, detection, attribution, security and intervention methods to keep deployed agent populations safe at scale. Proposals may target one cluster or span several, with priority on depth over breadth and on realistic sandboxes and testbeds. OUT OF SCOPE: single-agent safety, capability advancement without a clear safety motivation, toy systems, naive application of pre-existing solutions, commercial product development, and purely conceptual/policy work. ELIGIBILITY: individual researchers, research teams, research institutions and multi-institution collaborations across universities, national laboratories, institutes and nonprofit research organisations; open globally. Informational webinars on 30 June and 23 July 2026; notification of decisions in autumn 2026. Proposals due 8 August 2026 by 11:59pm Anywhere on Earth. Contact: multiagentsafety@schmidtsciences.org. Apply at https://schmidtsciences.smapply.io/prog/scaling_ai_safety_for_a_multi_agent_world/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
